phases.title: Phase 01 - Core Engine
phases.description: Builds the game loop, input handling, rendering basics, collision foundations, ECS structure, and debug visibility.

phases.title: Phase 02 - World + Visual
phases.description: Expands into cameras, assets, animation, prefabs, tilemaps, serialization, and playable world slices.

phases.title: Phase 03 - Gameplay Systems
phases.description: Introduces movement rules, interaction systems, combat foundations, hazards, pickups, NPCs, and enemy behavior patterns.

phases.title: Phase 04 - Camera + Feel
phases.description: Focuses on camera behavior and input feel, covering dead zones, smoothing, shake, buffering, queues, and timing windows.

phases.title: Phase 05 - UI + Systems
phases.description: Builds combat-facing UI and reusable support systems such as events, state machines, config loading, debugging, and metrics.

phases.title: Phase 06 - AI + World Simulation
phases.description: Explores navigation, AI behaviors, questing, scripting, cutscenes, spawning, weather, and broader world simulation features.

phases.title: Phase 07 - Polish + Scale
phases.description: Improves usability and production readiness with remapping, controller support, optimization, saves, replays, localization, and packaging.

phases.title: Phase 08 - Platform + UX
phases.description: Rounds out audio, UI framework work, scene flow, profile handling, and platform-facing error and logging support.

phases.title: Phase 09 - Graphics + Precision Collision
phases.description: Moves into advanced rendering and collision precision with vector, polygon, mask, pixel-perfect, and hybrid approaches.

phases.title: Phase 10 - Platform Utilities
phases.description: Covers platform utility features for audio playback, browser storage helpers, and playlist management.

phases.title: Phase 11 - Release + Deployment
phases.description: Prepares projects for shipping with settings, accessibility, packaging, deployment profiles, and release validation workflows.

phases.title: Phase 12 - Demo Games
phases.description: Playable demo-game track for hero movement, jump timing, camera follow, and layered world presentation.

phases.title: Phase 13 - Network Concepts, Latency and Simulation
phases.description: Introduces networking concepts using local and simulated systems (event bus, input models, latency simulation).

phases.title: Phase 14 - Editor + Automation + Trust + Pipeline
phases.description: Builds authoring tools, automated validation, CI flows, trust checks, and import pipelines for a reliable workflow.

phases.title: Phase 15 - Advanced Systems
phases.description: Introduces deterministic scheduling, replay-friendly state handling, diffing, and verification systems.

samples.title: Basic Loop
samples.description: Demonstrates the core engine loop with simple bounded motion.

samples.title: Keyboard Move
samples.description: Demonstrates keyboard-driven movement inside a bounded play area.

samples.title: Mouse Input
samples.description: Demonstrates mouse input tracking and renderer-based feedback.

samples.title: Gamepad Input
samples.description: Demonstrates basic gamepad input and live control feedback.

samples.title: Scene Switch
samples.description: Demonstrates scene transitions and shared engine scene flow.

samples.title: Input Mapping
samples.description: Demonstrates action-based input mapping using shared bindings.

samples.title: Entity Movement
samples.description: Demonstrates entity movement driven by shared engine input patterns.

samples.title: Render Adapter
samples.description: Demonstrates renderer abstraction through the render adapter.

samples.title: Collision
samples.description: Demonstrates axis-aligned bounding box collision detection with visual debug feedback and keyboard-controlled movement.

samples.title: Collision Response
samples.description: Demonstrates single-block collision response by stopping player movement at the obstacle instead of allowing overlap.

samples.title: Multiple Solids
samples.description: Demonstrates collision response against several blocking rectangles using one shared collision loop.

samples.title: Axis Separated Collision
samples.description: Demonstrates separate X and Y collision resolution so the player can slide cleanly along blocking surfaces.

samples.title: Tile Collision
samples.description: Demonstrates tile-based collision by treating selected grid cells as solid blockers with axis-separated movement.

samples.title: Collision Debug Tools
samples.description: Demonstrates collision inspection tools such as candidate highlights, nearest blocker emphasis, and live overlap status text.

samples.title: ECS Foundation
samples.description: Demonstrates a minimal entity-component world with reusable component storage and renderer-driven drawing.

samples.title: ECS Movement System
samples.description: Demonstrates an ECS movement system that updates entity positions from transform and velocity components.

samples.title: ECS Input System
samples.description: Demonstrates an ECS input system that controls one entity through input and speed components.

samples.title: ECS Collision System
samples.description: Demonstrates an ECS collision system that blocks a controlled entity against solids using collider and solid components.

samples.title: ECS Render System
samples.description: Demonstrates a dedicated ECS render pass that draws multiple entities from transform, size, and renderable components.

samples.title: ECS Scene World
samples.description: Demonstrates a scene that delegates interaction to ECS-style systems for input, movement, collision, and rendering.

samples.title: UI Overlay
samples.description: HUD and debug overlay rendered separately from world.

samples.title: Entity Lifecycle
samples.description: Spawning, collecting, and removing entities.

samples.title: Debug Stats
samples.description: FPS, entity counts, debug toggle.

samples.title: Data Driven World
samples.description: Scene built from config data.

samples.title: Camera Follow
samples.description: Demonstrates a larger world with a camera that follows the player as the viewport scrolls across the map.

samples.title: Camera Bounds
samples.description: Demonstrates camera follow with camera clamping so the viewport never shows beyond the world edges.

samples.title: Minimap
samples.description: Demonstrates a minimap overlay that shows world bounds, player position, and obstacle placement at a reduced scale.

samples.title: Asset Registry
samples.description: Demonstrates a simple asset registry pattern for centralizing image, audio, and data references before real loading is added.

samples.title: Save Load State
samples.description: Demonstrates saving and restoring player state with browser storage so a scene can persist position between actions.

samples.title: Level Loader
samples.description: Demonstrates loading a level definition from structured data so scene content is configured outside the scene class.

samples.title: Animation System
samples.description: Demonstrates animation state playback with idle and move loops driven by the hardened engine.

samples.title: State Machine
samples.description: Demonstrates state-driven behavior using a reusable engine state machine.

samples.title: Interaction System
samples.description: Demonstrates prompts and interactions using engine-owned interaction helpers.

samples.title: Projectile System
samples.description: Demonstrates runtime spawning and updating of projectiles using shared engine projectile helpers.

samples.title: Enemy Patrol AI
samples.description: Demonstrates a patrol behavior and player detection using engine AI helpers.

samples.title: Playable Micro Level
samples.description: Combines the hardened engine pieces into a small playable slice with interaction, enemy patrol, goal, and projectiles.

samples.title: Sprite Render Layer
samples.description: Demonstrates a sprite-ready render path with color-rect fallback using the engine render layer.

samples.title: Animation Sprite Binding
samples.description: Demonstrates binding animation state to sprite-frame-like render output.

samples.title: Z Layer Ordering
samples.description: Demonstrates layered draw ordering for background, world, actors, projectiles, UI, and debug.

samples.title: Prefab System
samples.description: Demonstrates prefab-based entity creation for players, enemies, pickups, and projectiles.

samples.title: Game Mode State
samples.description: Demonstrates higher-level game mode flow for title, play, pause, win, and lose states.

samples.title: Polished Playable Slice
samples.description: Combines prefabs, render layers, game modes, interaction, AI, and projectiles into a cleaner mini-game slice.

samples.title: Sprite Atlas Image Rendering
samples.description: Demonstrates sprite-atlas-style frame lookup and image-loader-ready rendering proxies.

samples.title: Camera System
samples.description: Demonstrates a world-space camera system so scenes stop doing ad hoc offset math.

samples.title: Tilemap System
samples.description: Demonstrates tilemap rendering and tile-based collision as the foundation for map-driven games.

samples.title: Input Action Mapping
samples.description: Demonstrates action-based input mapping so gameplay is decoupled from physical keys.

samples.title: World Serialization
samples.description: Demonstrates saving and restoring a world snapshot through shared serialization helpers.

samples.title: Tile Camera Sprite Slice
samples.description: Combines tilemap, camera, action input, sprite-atlas-style rendering, and world serialization into a playable slice.

samples.title: Real Sprite Rendering
samples.description: Makes image-backed sprite rendering the standard actor path instead of color-box placeholders.

samples.title: Animation System
samples.description: Adds stable timing, loop control, and a one-shot action animation on top of the real sprite path.

samples.title: Physics System
samples.description: Introduces reusable velocity, acceleration, and drag based movement for a more game-ready motion model.

samples.title: Collision Resolution
samples.description: Improves tile collision response with axis-separated resolution and better wall sliding behavior.

samples.title: Tile Metadata
samples.description: Adds hazard, trigger, and slope-style metadata definitions to the tilemap system.

samples.title: Nes Style Zones Parallax
samples.description: Demonstrates section-based camera zones and simple parallax layers inspired by classic side scrollers.

samples.title: Slopes Ramps
samples.description: First-pass slope and ramp sample. Move across the purple ramp surfaces to verify the actor can climb an angled floor instead of colliding as if it were a wall.

samples.title: Gravity Zones
samples.description: Colored zones change the direction of gravity. Move through the blue, orange, and green fields to verify that gravity can be authored per-zone instead of globally. Press Space to jump opposite the current gravity direction.

samples.title: Ladders Climb Zones
samples.description: Yellow ladder volumes let the actor climb vertically and suspend falling. Enter a ladder, climb up to the next platform, and step off to resume normal gravity.

samples.title: Moving Platforms
samples.description: Two moving platforms demonstrate ride/carry behavior. Stand on the cyan platforms and verify that the actor is translated with the platform instead of slipping off every frame.

samples.title: One Way Platforms
samples.description: Orange platforms only collide when the actor falls onto them from above. Jump upward through them, land on them from above, and press Down + Space to drop through.

samples.title: Friction Surfaces
samples.description: Blue ice and brown mud surfaces alter horizontal movement feel. Compare momentum retention and traction as you move across each authored surface strip.

samples.title: Trigger Zones
samples.description: Demonstrates enter and exit trigger zones. Walk through the colored fields to see one-time enter events, active states, and exit messages without requiring button presses.

samples.title: Pickups Collectibles
samples.description: Demonstrates collectibles that disappear when touched, increment score, and can require jumping to reach elevated items.

samples.title: Switches Buttons
samples.description: Demonstrates floor buttons and toggled switches. Contact with the red floor pad and interact with the yellow wall switch to change linked state.

samples.title: Doors Gates
samples.description: Demonstrates linked buttons and a door gate. Step on the red button to open the center gate and walk through before it closes again.

samples.title: Basic NPC Entity
samples.description: Demonstrates a basic NPC entity with patrol motion, facing direction, and a simple speech bubble when the player gets close.

samples.title: Interaction Press Key
samples.description: Demonstrates a generic press-key interaction system. Walk up to the console, see the prompt, and press E to toggle the target state.

samples.title: Hitboxes Hurtboxes
samples.description: Demonstrates the difference between an attack hitbox and a hurtbox. Move the player into range and press Space to test overlap.

samples.title: Projectile System
samples.description: Introduces spawned projectiles with travel, lifetime, cleanup, and simple hit detection. Press F to fire.

samples.title: Health System
samples.description: Adds health values, simple damage application, HUD readout, and reset behavior for combat testing.

samples.title: Damage Knockback
samples.description: Adds directional knockback when damage lands so hits feel physical instead of purely numeric.

samples.title: Invulnerability Frames
samples.description: Prevents repeated damage every frame during overlap. The target flashes while temporarily invulnerable.

samples.title: Simple Enemy AI
samples.description: Adds a simple enemy that idles until the player comes near, then follows. Contact shows the first end-to-end mini combat loop.

samples.title: Camera Dead Zone
samples.description: Demonstrates a dead zone where the camera waits before following the actor.

samples.title: Camera Look Ahead
samples.description: Demonstrates look-ahead camera movement that anticipates actor direction.

samples.title: Camera Smoothing
samples.description: Demonstrates smoothed camera movement instead of a hard snap follow.

samples.title: Camera Bounds
samples.description: Demonstrates clamped camera travel inside world bounds.

samples.title: Camera Shake
samples.description: Demonstrates a simple camera shake effect triggered during play.

samples.title: Camera Zoom
samples.description: Demonstrates the same actor-centered world at multiple camera zoom levels.

samples.title: Input Buffering
samples.description: Demonstrates queued input that executes when the action becomes valid.

samples.title: Input Queue Priority
samples.description: Demonstrates queued actions resolving by configured priority.

samples.title: Input Timing Windows
samples.description: Demonstrates valid input only during configured timing windows.

samples.title: Action Cooldowns
samples.description: Demonstrates reusable cooldown timers for actions.

samples.title: Input Chaining
samples.description: Demonstrates named input sequences triggering chained actions.

samples.title: Input State Debug Overlay
samples.description: Demonstrates a live debug overlay for action input states.

samples.title: Hitboxes Hurtboxes
samples.description: Demonstrates reusable attack overlap regions and target hurtboxes.

samples.title: Attack Timing Windows

samples.title: Damage Invulnerability
samples.description: Demonstrates damage application with temporary invulnerability after a hit.

samples.title: Knockback Response
samples.description: Demonstrates reusable directional knockback when damage lands.

samples.title: Health Death State
samples.description: Demonstrates health reduction to zero and a reusable dead disabled state.

samples.title: Combat Debug Overlay
samples.description: Demonstrates an engine-owned overlay for hitboxes, hurtboxes, timing, health, and invulnerability.

samples.title: Event Bus

samples.title: Tilemap Viewer
samples.description: Renders a stable tilemap larger than the viewport. Use Arrow keys to pan the viewer camera.

samples.title: Tilemap Hero Movement
samples.description: Use Left/Right Arrow to move the hero. The camera follows horizontally across a wide tilemap.

samples.title: Tilemap Hero Jump Collision
samples.description: Move with Left/Right and jump with Space. Gravity and tile collisions drive grounded landing behavior.

samples.title: Tilemap Parallax Hero
samples.description: Move with Left/Right and jump with Space. Camera-relative parallax layers add background depth while gameplay remains focused on platform traversal.

samples.title: Multi-System Demo
samples.description: Move with Left/Right and jump with Space. Collect the pickups while traversal, collision, camera follow, and parallax remain active. Debug is production-safe and opt-in: use ?debug=1 (and optionally &debugMode=qa ) to enable console/overlay.

samples.title: Trigger Zone
samples.description: Move with Left/Right and jump with Space. Reach the trigger zone to activate success state.

samples.description: Move with Left/Right and jump with Space. Touch the activator to toggle checkpoint state and marker visuals.

samples.description: Move with Left/Right and jump with Space across a scrolling world rendered from tool-formatted tile and parallax assets.

samples.description: Filters world state so each client sees only nearby or relevant entities.

samples.description: Exposes correction and replay behavior for prediction diagnostics.

samples.title: Network Sample A
samples.description: Sample-backed network diagnostics with no real transport. This validates read-only providers, passive overlay panels, and operator-safe command surfaces before host/client wiring.
samples.indexLabel: Network Sample A - Local Loopback / Fake Network

samples.title: Network Sample B
samples.description: Sample-backed multi-role diagnostics that simulate host and client peers with read-only debug surfaces and no real transport layer.
samples.indexLabel: Network Sample B - Host / Client Diagnostics

samples.title: Sample 1318 Network Commands and Trace Validation
samples.description: Deterministic mismatch and trace-validation showcase with timeline sequencing, divergence explanation notes, reproduction guidance, and checklist-driven verification.
samples.indexLabel: Network Sample C - Divergence / Trace Validation
